entry.title: Revolutionising SAP S/4HANA Sales: Unleashing Intelligent Order Management Powered by Joule & AI
entry.content:
In today's fast-paced business landscape, where customers demand seamless experiences and personalized interactions, companies are increasingly turning to advanced technologies to streamline their operations and enhance customer satisfaction. One such technology revolutionizing the sales domain is SAP S/4HANA Sales coupled with Artificial Intelligence (AI). This innovative combination is transforming traditional order management processes into intelligent, data-driven operations that deliver significant improvements in efficiency, accuracy, and customer satisfaction.

First, Let’s See how the traditional Sales process works and its challenges…

The sales order process is the heartbeat of any organization. It directly impacts customer relationships, revenue generation, and overall business performance. However, traditional sales order processing techniques often present challenges, especially due to incomplete sales order data. Let’s get into some of the pain points faced by businesses:

  1. Manual Data Entry Errors: When sales representatives manually input data into systems, human error is inevitable. Missing or inaccurate information can lead to shipping delays, improper product deliveries, and negative customer experiences.
  2. Time-Consuming Data Validation: Verifying and cross-referencing data from different sources consumes valuable time. Sales teams often spend excessive hours validating customer details, product codes, and pricing information.
  3. Order Processing Delays: Incomplete data not only slows down order processing but can also result in order rejection or cancellation. Such delays create friction in customer relationships and impact the company’s credibility.
  4. Customer Frustration: Customers expect a seamless buying experience. Delays caused by incomplete data can frustrate customers, leading to dissatisfaction and potential loss of business.

Let’s explore & see, how SAP S/4HANA intelligent Order management is a game changer for this situation …

SAP S/4HANA Sales, provides businesses with a comprehensive platform for managing sales order fulfillment. When integrated with AI capabilities, such as machine learning and natural language processing, SAP S/4HANA Sales becomes even more powerful, enabling organizations to analyze vast amounts of data, automate repetitive tasks, and make data-driven decisions in real time.

Here, we need to understand the impact of Joule on business processes : 

What is Joule –

Joule is a transformative natural-language, generative AI copilot designed to revolutionize the way businesses operate within SAP’s cloud portfolio. 

  • It’s an AI companion
  • It’s embedded throughout SAP’s cloud applications.
  • It’s a trusted AI chatbot that enhances daily operations.

How it works :

  • Joule leverages Gen AI capabilities
  • It learns the pattern from existing data and proposes intelligent insight.

How customers use Joule :

  • Joule can categorize and contextualize information from multiple sources.
  • It assists users in various ways, such as executing finance operations or changing sales document entries.
  • It boosts productivity by granting quick and seamless access to user enablement content.

Let’s see a glimpse of the Intelligent Order Management process in SAP S/4HANA –

Fig 1 : Manage Sales Order : Version 2

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

The user creates/searches for sales orders in the new "Manage Sales Order: Version 2" app.  This app is enhanced with Microsoft Teams integration. This functionality allows users to share business info with related colleagues with screen data. 

Fig 2: Microsoft Teams IntegrationFig 3: Sharing Business InfoFig 4: Shared info with Screen data

 

The User now can activate/check "Joule". Joule can be immensely helpful to users with all business-related queries. With some continuous prompts, users can get their required business info/guidance to proceed further. In this case, the user has asked for information about the possibility of extracting information from PDF. With subsequent prompts, Joule has guided the user directly to navigate the Sales order auto extraction app to create a sales order by uploading a PDF. Refer to my blog How to create sales orders from PDF data? 

Fig 5: Activate JouleFig 6: Joule's Response to PromptFig 7: Subsequent prompt to JouleFig 8: Response based on PromptFig 9: Create Sales order : AutoExtraction appFig 10: Upload PDF data to create OrderFig 11: Validate and create sales order

 

Now in case of wrong data in PDF upload or due to human error, the newly created sales order goes to "incompletion" mode. Here also embedded AI helps users to autocomplete sales orders and proceed further. For more details, please refer to my blog Sales Order AutoComplete with AI  

Fig 12 : Sales Order AutoComplete Notification and status

 

Customer Benefits and Process Improvement with Joule and AI-driven Approach:

  • Enhanced Efficiency and Accuracy

By leveraging AI-driven automation and predictive analytics, businesses can streamline sales operations, reduce manual effort, and minimize errors. Automated order processing and intelligent inventory management ensure faster order fulfillment, reduced lead times, and improved order accuracy.

  • Real-time Decision Making

SAP S/4HANA Sales integrated with AI provides real-time visibility into sales performance, inventory levels, and customer interactions. This empowers businesses to make data-driven decisions on pricing, inventory management, and resource allocation, enabling them to respond swiftly to changing market dynamics and customer demands.

  • Return on Investment (ROI) and Customer Satisfaction

The implementation of SAP S/4HANA Sales with AI capabilities results in significant cost savings through improved operational efficiency, reduced inventory carrying costs, and optimized resource utilization. Moreover, AI-driven pricing optimization and personalized recommendations drive revenue growth by increasing sales volumes, improving pricing margins, and enhancing customer retention.

  • Competitive Advantage and Market Differentiation

Businesses that embrace SAP S/4HANA Sales coupled with AI gain a competitive advantage by offering superior customer experiences, faster order fulfillment, and more accurate pricing. This positions them as industry leaders and enhances their brand reputation, attracting new customers and retaining existing ones.

The integration of SAP S/4HANA Sales with Artificial Intelligence is revolutionizing the sales domain by enabling businesses to transform traditional order management processes into intelligent, data-driven operations. As organizations embrace digital transformation, AI-driven order management becomes indispensable. Joule exemplifies how AI enhances efficiency, accuracy, and customer satisfaction. By leveraging intelligent technologies, businesses unlock new levels of success in their sales processes. In summary, the marriage of AI and order management in SAP S/4HANA isn’t just a technological leap; it’s a strategic move toward sustainable growth and customer delight.

entry.title: SAP User Experience in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2402
entry.content:
Welcome to the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2402 release. Our fully-fledged cloud ERP is the foundation of the intelligent, sustainable enterprise and offers innovation and enhancements beyond measure. But even the most scalable cloud ERP would only be half as efficient without a seamless user experience. Therefore, we keep enhancing our product based on your feedback to offer you a next-level user experience so that you can thrive in today’s work environment. It is my pleasure to show you just how we do that in this release. Spoiler alert: Generative AI is involved. 😉

Check out my video below to get a first glimpse into what’s coming in the area of SAP User Experience in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2402:

Horizon Theme

With SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2208, we introduced the Horizon visual theme to offer you a fresh and modern SAP Fiori experience and to help you get your job done faster, more intuitively, and with better outcomes. Taking your feedback to heart, we’ve already made continuous improvements to the design in past releases. With this new release, we now implemented even more changes such as:

  • White input fields instead of gray,
  • Clearly identifiable buttons,
  • Improved contrast,
  • Reduced header size,
  • And more.

Read more about this in Thomas Reiss’s blog post.

Figure 1: The darker background on the My Home is only one of many design improvements to the Horizon visual theme.

Spaces and Pages

As announced in my previous blog post, the groups setup on the SAP Fiori launchpad is now officially outdated; spaces and pages are the only available layout option as of this release (read more in this note). Switching to spaces and pages has now become crucial to avoid a blank screen on the SAP Fiori launchpad–and note, the "switch" does not happen automatically, but has to be done manually. Read this blog post by Sylvie Charpentier on how to efficiently switch to spaces and pages. We are granting a grace period until June 2024 to all customers who haven’t switched to spaces and pages yet. During this grace period, there’s no guarantee that the group-based content is up to date. At the end of this grace period, SAP will change the settings in development and test systems first, and eventually also in production systems to display spaces and pages as default without the option for administrators and business users to switch back to groups. The administrators of affected customers will be informed about these changes in due time.

With SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2402, we ship a new functionality to merge spaces and pages which can be used to e.g., reduce the number of duplicate apps, pages, or spaces for users. This can also be helpful if you want to enrich SAP-delivered spaces or pages with customer-defined content.

Figure 2: “Accounts Payable Accountant” and “Accounts Payable Manager” before merging.

Figure 3: View after merging “Accounts Payable Accountant” and “Accounts Payable Manager”.

Find out more in Kattia Jordan-Philipp's blog post.

My Home in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

Switching to spaces and pages will not only give you a new layout option to structure the access to apps following work processes in an intuitive and efficient way; it also enables you to use the My Home entry page in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. Users benefit from a personalized interface tailored to their needs, with important to-dos and insights readily accessible. Designed for efficiency across devices, the My Home ensures quick task execution through intuitive app organization. With the 2402 release, we offer an increased level of personalization to make the user experience even more straightforward. This includes:

  • The harmonization of blank state,
  • Consistent group creation via drag and drop,
  • A total re-design of the app section,
  • And an increased language support for system news.

For new customers, spaces and pages and My Home are the default and you will benefit from them immediately.

Figure 4: Discover the latest enhancements to the My Home in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2402. Click on the image to watch the video.

Collaborative ERP

The My Home in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition offers a new collaboration feature with Microsoft Teams. Based on contact information in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, you can now directly trigger a Microsoft Teams chat or voice/video call and start collaborating.

Figure 5: The integration between My Home and Microsoft Teams allows you to easily collaborate across apps. Click on the image to watch the video.

And there’s more: Another feature of the collaborative ERP is now generally available! We enable you to share your most relevant business data using cards in Microsoft Teams chats. Through the cards, recipients of the message can directly edit the data in the collaborative stage view in Microsoft Teams embedded in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

Figure 6: Share business data using cards in Microsoft Teams chats and start collaborating. Click the image to watch the video.

SAP Collaboration Manager

With SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2302, we introduced the SAP Collaboration Manager. This cloud-based, innovative collaboration feature replaced the human-to-human chat function of the former SAP CoPilot in the three-system landscape and offers a seamlessly integrated collaboration experience directly inside the SAP Fiori launchpad, increasing overall user efficiency. Since then, we’ve been continuously enhancing the SAP Collaboration Manager to offer more capabilities such as creating chats and inviting your co-workers to collaborate without any system switch over, taking screenshots and annotating or highlighting them, and linking your chat to any screen for reference. In this release, we offer even more enhancements:

  • Add My Home “Insights” cards,
  • Mark notes and chats as “Read” or “Unread”,
  • Add notes and chats to “Favorites”.

Figure 7: The “Add Insights Cards” function allows you to provide convenient access to analytical information, lists, graphs, and associated apps.

SAPUI5 Adaptation Projects

As of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2402, developers can work with SAPUI5 Adaptation Projects in SAP Business Application Studio (SAP BAS) to create variants of standard applications. In an adaptation project, you can extend the original code to define changes that fulfill the specific business requirements of different business roles, user groups, organizational units, and countries. For example, you might want to simplify and streamline a specific process by hiding fields not required by casual users, visualizing data as a chart, or changing a responsive table to a grid table to make information easier to process. After deployment, both the original application and your adapted application exist in parallel and can be made available to the relevant users through uniquely name application tiles in SAP Fiori launchpad/My Home.

Read more about this in Elisabeth Riemann's blog post.

Figure 8: You can now create SAPUI5 Adaptation Projects in SAP Business Application Studio to generate app variants. Click the image to watch the video.

Joule for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2402

In my first paragraph of this blog post, I promised to talk about generative AI–so, let’s talk about SAP’s brand-new, natural language genAI copilot, Joule. Joule is a comprehensive solution designed to understand the complexities of your business processes and will completely revolutionize how you interact with the system. It provides transparent, role-based access to relevant processes, making interactions intuitive and seamless. By enabling natural conversations between users and systems, Joule enhances productivity and delivers valuable insights. The three supported conversational patterns are:

  • Navigational: Joule helps users find required functionalities within SAP systems,
  • Transactional: Joule assists users in completing tasks,
  • Informational: Joule provides insights based on SAP Help documentation data.

With SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2402, Joule is taking off through an Early Adopter Care program and will be available for selected customers for initial testing. Read more about Joule and other AI innovations in this blog post by Syed Talal Hussain.

Figure 9: SAP’s generative AI copilot Joule revolutionizes how you interact with the system. Click the image to watch the video.

Customer Influence Page

Our top priority has always been ensuring a seamless and straightforward user experience. Reacting to your feedback and requirements is the key to achieving this. The main input channel where we receive your requirements is the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, User Experience and Cross-Topics customer influence page. To make sure we collect all your requests, we emphasize the importance of the channel and increase the threshold to 10 to get a better focus. We review permanently and stop all improvement requests that are older than 18 months and have not (yet) met the current voting threshold. Based on UX importance, here is a status report of features that were delivered and that are still in process/planning stage:

  • S4HC Home page personalization: User CB ID to be shown under “User Account” menu (19 votes), 2nd most-voted improvement request in the session
    • Delivered with current release
  • Adapt UI – Enable ability to set field properties (i.e., mandatory, visible) (15 votes), 4th most-voted improvement request in the session
    • Can be achieved through UI Adaptation Projects
  • Upload custom logo without having to use UI Theme Designer (2TV) (13 votes), 6th most-voted improvement request in the session
    • Planned with next release (Q3/2024)
  • Enable a download/upload of the My Home app tile information (11 votes), 11th  most-voted improvement request in the session
    • Delivered with current release

Figure 10: Our customer influence page is the main channel to request requirements.

When I said we are offering next-level user experience with this new release, I wasn’t promising too much. Get more details about the innovations and enhancements mentioned in this blog post and much, much more in Thomas Reiss’s blog post.

Feel free to drop a kudo if you liked this blog post. Don’t hesitate to leave a comment and check out our new community page to ask your questions and engage with the experts. Follow the PSCC_Enablement tag to stay up to date with our latest blog posts. Looking forward to the next release!

Follow us on @SAP and #S4HANA, and myself on LinkedIn and X.

📽️ Watch Our Live Sessions

In January 2024, we hosted a series of 19 compelling live sessions to highlight the exciting innovations shipped with the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2402 release. Missed the live sessions? No problem! Take advantage of the entire series or pick and choose the sessions most relevant to you on demand.

 

🎙️ Inside SAP S/4HANA Cloud

There is no customer success without project success and product success! Leverage the unique knowledge and expertise of SAP S/4HANA Cloud experts, partners, and customers, sharing their SAP S/4HANA Cloud implementation project best practices and lessons learned – anywhere, anytime. In this podcast, we give you all ingredients to get to the next level and make your SAP S/4HANA Cloud project a success. Subscribe now and benefit from the shared knowledge!

 

💻openSAP Microlearnings for SAP S/4HANA Cloud

Our openSAP microlearnings for SAP S/4HANA Cloud offer an exciting new learning opportunity. What began with a small batch of 20 videos, has now become a channel with more than 50 microlearnings that have generated over 20,000 views. Today, we cover multiple lines of business such as finance, manufacturing, and supply chain management, and key technology topics like Master Data Management, extensibility, SAP User Experience, and upgrade management. We are continuously adding new microlearnings to our channel, so make sure you check them out.

 

🗣️ Your Voice Matters

You want to learn more and actively engage with SAP subject matter experts on SAP S/4HANA Cloud topics? We have just the right place for you! Join our interactive community where we bring together customers, partners, and SAP experts with a clear mission: To engage with one another about best practices and product solutions. We invite you to explore the ‘one-stop shop’ as the central place for all resources, tools, content questions, answers and to connect with experts who will guide you through your journey to the intelligent, sustainable enterprise.

 

🔎Find All of Our Assets

SAP S/4HANA Cloud is the foundation of the intelligent, sustainable enterprise and is an innovative, robust, and scalable ERP. We at Cloud ERP Product Success and Cloud Co-Innovation offer a service as versatile as our product itself. Check out the numerous offerings our team has created for you below:

entry.title: Exploring the Business Benefits of AI
entry.content:
Artificial Intelligence (AI) has emerged as a transformative technology that is revolutionizing various industries. From automating routine tasks to enabling personalized customer experiences, AI is driving significant business benefits across diverse sectors.

There are numerous advantages that AI brings to businesses, highlighting its potential to enhance efficiency, productivity, decision-making, and customer satisfaction.

Enhanced efficiency and productivity

One of the key advantages of AI is its ability to automate repetitive and time-consuming tasks. By leveraging machine learning algorithms, businesses can streamline processes, reduce human error, and improve overall efficiency. AI-powered automation can handle tasks such as data entry, data analysis, customer support, and inventory management, freeing up human resources to focus on more strategic and creative endeavors. This increased efficiency leads to higher productivity, cost savings, and improved resource allocation.

Data-driven decision-making

AI has the potential to unlock valuable insights from vast amounts of data. By analyzing patterns, trends, and correlations, AI algorithms can generate actionable intelligence that aids in decision-making. Businesses can use AI to optimize pricing strategies, forecast demand, identify market trends, and personalize marketing campaigns. With AI-powered analytics, organizations can make data-driven decisions that are more accurate, timely, and aligned with their business goals.

Improved customer experience

AI technologies, such as natural language processing and machine learning, have revolutionized customer service. Chatbots and virtual assistants can provide instant and personalized responses to customer queries, improving response times and enhancing overall customer experience. AI-powered recommendation systems can analyze customer preferences and behaviors to offer personalized product recommendations, leading to increased customer satisfaction and loyalty. Moreover, AI can help businesses gain a deeper understanding of their customers through sentiment analysis, social media monitoring, and customer segmentation, enabling targeted marketing and better customer relationship management.

Enhanced cybersecurity

As businesses increasingly rely on digital infrastructure, cybersecurity has become a critical concern. AI can play a pivotal role in safeguarding sensitive data and detecting potential threats. Machine learning algorithms can analyze network traffic patterns, identify anomalies, and proactively respond to security breaches. AI-powered systems can also continuously learn and adapt to new threats, making them an invaluable asset in the fight against cybercrime.

Streamlined operations and supply chain management

AI can optimize various aspects of operations and supply chain management. Predictive analytics can forecast demand, allowing businesses to optimize inventory levels and reduce wastage. AI-powered algorithms can also optimize production schedules, logistics routes, and delivery processes, leading to cost savings and improved efficiency. By harnessing AI, businesses can streamline their operations, minimize downtime, and ensure timely delivery, thereby gaining a competitive edge.

The business benefits of AI are vast and diverse. From enhancing efficiency and productivity to enabling data-driven decision-making and improving customer experiences, AI is transforming the way businesses operate. By embracing AI technologies, organizations can gain a competitive advantage, drive innovation, and unlock new opportunities for growth. As AI continues to evolve, it is crucial for businesses to explore and harness its potential to stay ahead in today's dynamic and competitive marketplace.

SAP gives customers over 130 AI capabilities embedded into SAP applications, empowering their business processes with AI that's as intuitive as it is flexible and powerful. Tailored to our customers’ unique data landscape and industry nuances, our AI enables smarter decisions and efficiencies at scale, while our unwavering commitment to responsibility ensures trust and compliance in every interaction.

Interesting in learning more?

  • Read about SAP’s Business AI strategy
  • Meet Joule, SAP’s AI co-pilot
  • What is generative AI?

entry.title: 5 Game-Changing Capabilities in Generative AI for Business
entry.content:
It’s no surprise that generative artificial intelligence (GenAI) is starting to have a profound impact on businesses. With its ability to unlock valuable insights, help make better decisions, automate tasks, and personalize experiences, the use cases are endless. As businesses increasingly recognize the potential of GenAI, key capabilities have emerged, reshaping lines of business and industries, and opening up new avenues for innovation and growth. 

Let’s take a look at five capabilities in GenAI that are changing the way businesses are run.  

1. Enhanced content creation: 

GenAI has transformed content creation by automating and streamlining the process. Businesses can now leverage AI algorithms to generate high-quality images, videos, and written content, saving time and resources while ensuring a consistent flow of high-quality content. 
 
2. Personalized customer experiences: 

Hyper-personalized customer experiences are now easily obtainable with GenAI. By analyzing vast amounts of data, AI algorithms can generate tailored recommendations, product suggestions, targeted campaigns, and more. This results in enhanced customer satisfaction and increased brand loyalty. 

3. Virtual assistants and copilots: 

The capabilities of digital assistants have become significantly advanced due to GenAI. These AI-powered tools can now engage in more natural and human-like conversations, understanding context and providing accurate responses – more akin to a copilot that understands every aspect of your business. Copilots can interact with customers, answer queries, perform tasks, provide personalized recommendations, handle customer support, and even streamline various business processes. 

 4. Simulation and optimization:

GenAI can simulate complex business processes and optimize them for maximum efficiency. By modeling different scenarios and variables, it can identify the most effective strategies and optimize processes accordingly, such as optimizing supply chain operations, logistics, resource allocation, or even financial forecasting. This can help businesses minimize risks, improve performance, and achieve better outcomes. 

5. Responsible and ethical AI: 

As GenAI becomes more pervasive, ethical considerations are becoming increasingly important. Businesses focus on developing and implementing frameworks for responsible AI usage. This includes addressing biases in AI algorithms, ensuring fairness and inclusivity, and establishing guidelines for AI governance. Organizations that prioritize ethical and responsible AI practices will not only mitigate risks but also build trust with customers and stakeholders.

Watch the video: AI Built into All Core Business Processes

As businesses continue to embrace GenAI, they can harness its potential to gain a competitive edge, drive innovation, and deliver exceptional value to customers. The future of AI technology holds immense promise, and its continued evolution will undoubtedly shape the business landscape well into the future. 

SAP gives customers AI capabilities embedded into SAP applications, empowering their business processes with AI that's as intuitive as it is flexible and powerful. Tailored to our customers’ unique data landscape and industry nuances, our AI enables smarter decisions and efficiencies at scale, while our unwavering commitment to responsibility ensures trust and compliance in every interaction. 

Interesting in learning more?

  • Learn more about SAP Business AI
  • Meet Joule, SAP’s AI copilot
  • What is generative AI?

entry.title: SAP BTP Ask Me Anything Series: SAP Build Code
entry.content:
Coming up next on the SAP BTP Ask Me Anything series: SAP Build Code. Get ready to have your questions answered live by our experts on March 27th – get all details about the session in this blog post. 

SAP Build Code was announced at SAP TechEd 2023 as THE game-changer for SAP developers. Why? Because it provides them with a seamlessly integrated environment for coding, testing, and application lifecycle management. And all that with generative AI-based code development with Joule copilot, based on natural language descriptions, and optimized for Java and JavaScript.  

What's it all about?  

With SAP Build Code, you get guided experiences and prebuilt templates to quickly build applications and extend your SAP systems - watch the teaser demo. It combines the most essential services from SAP BTP and delivers a cohesive experience for developers and fusion development teams:

Fusion development is all about breaking silos between teams and empowering business and IT to collaborate successfully – and this is what combining SAP Build Code and the low-code tools in SAP Build, can help you achieve. You can learn more about how SAP Build and SAP Build Code work together in this blog post: Comprehensive Tools for Novice to Expert Developers. 

Why should I join? 

SAP Build Code is now on preview and will be generally available in March 2024!! 

This upcoming AMA is the best option for you to learn everything you need to know about the solution, including how to get access and what’s next. And, of course, you’ll have your questions answered by experts in the topic: Tim Back, Chief Development Architect for SAP BTP Application Development, Automation and Integration, Rupa Maity is a Senior Product Manager and Yuval Anafi the Lead Product Owner for SAP Build Code. 

Join on March 27 – 3 pm CET:

🔔 Add calendar reminder

💻 Link to YouTube live

If you already have related questions, drop them in the comments below. We’ll bring them to the session, so our experts can answer them LIVE. 

See you there! 

All the best, 
Maiara  

Learn more about SAP BTP and SAP Build Code: 

SAP Build Code | Community Topic Page 

SAP BTP | Community Topic Page 

SAP BTP Innobytes: bite-sized monthly video series, sharing the latest hits and top SAP BTP innovations. 

What’s New with SAP BTP: quarterly LIVE series for SAP BTP innovation highlights, with demos and time for Q&A 

SAP BTP Garage: monthly LIVE webinar with hands-on use case walkthroughs and demos 

SAP BTP Talk: monthly podcast covering all things SAP BTP

entry.title: Navigating Business Transformation: Capgemini's Expertise in Data and AI Analytics with SAP
entry.content:
At Capgemini, we understand the evolving needs of businesses and the importance of using technology and innovation to stay ahead. Through our comprehensive approach encompassing Strategy and Transformation, Applications and Technology, Engineering, and Operations, we deliver tailored solutions to drive growth and efficiency.

 

Our SAP unit is strategically positioned at the forefront of data and AI/analytics integration, empowering our clients in their journey towards digital transformation. By leveraging SAP's robust cloud portfolio and integrated solution stack, we equip businesses, particularly the office of the CFO, with the tools needed to navigate the complexities of modern finance and beyond.

 

In a world where data and analytics are paramount, CFOs are increasingly tasked with overseeing the broader spectrum of business operations. Beyond traditional finance KPIs, they must focus on areas such as customer satisfaction, forward planning, and advanced data analytics. Recognizing this shift, Capgemini harnesses its deep industry knowledge to develop Line of Business (LoB) focused content centered around SAP Datasphere.

 

Our collaborative efforts extend beyond SAP, integrating cutting-edge partner technologies like Databricks and Google Cloud to provide end-to-end solutions for our clients. Through the development of an SAP + X reference architecture, we offer a solid foundation for data and analytics needs, tailored to the specific requirements of each organization.

 

The cornerstone of our approach lies in our ability to deliver tangible results quickly and effectively. By employing a "show early and fast" methodology, we engage clients from the outset, facilitating rapid requirement engineering and adjustment. This proactive approach ensures high user satisfaction and overall project success.

 

Our close partnership with SAP enables us to innovate based on evolving technologies while delivering seamless transitions from technology procurement to data-driven decision-making. Regular customer engagements, coupled with our extensive SAP business expertise, allow us to anticipate and address the evolving needs of our clients. Learn more about recent SAP Datasphere innovations in this blog posting from JG Chirapurath, CMO for Business Technology Platform, SAP   

 

At Capgemini, we recognize the strategic importance of a robust business data fabric and tight integration between SAP, its partners, and customers. SAP Datasphere, in conjunction with SAP Analytics Cloud and partner solutions, forms the bedrock for informed decision-making. We advocate for a centralized approach to SAP data, supplemented by hyperscale platforms and external data sources to provide a comprehensive view of business operations.

Capgemini’s commitment to excellence, coupled with our deep-rooted expertise in SAP and data analytics, positions us as a trusted partner in driving business transformation. Together, we navigate the complexities of the digital age, empowering organizations to thrive in an ever-evolving landscape.

 

Join us on the journey to unlock the full potential of your business with Capgemini and SAP.

entry.title: Revolutionizing Business through the Power of SAP’s Artificial Intelligence Superheroes
entry.content:
In recent years, we've seen a surge in interest and application of Artificial Intelligence (AI), machine learning, and data science. Generative AI has been making waves, and companies are eager to incorporate AI into all facets of their business processes. With the goal to lead in Business AI, SAP offers an extensive range of solutions from standalone AI services targeting a technical audience to embedded AI use cases in standard offerings, targeting users in the business departments.

While it makes perfect sense to provide different solutions, depending on the user group, business purpose or use case, this variety of options can be really confusing to someone who is new to the topic. But why do we offer so many AI options within the SAP portfolio? The answer is simple, sometimes it takes more than one superhero to save the world. In this blog post we want to share our personal experience and insights. We hope this analogy of AI infusing superhero powers to your business will help you better understand the breadth and depth of the SAP portfolio.

We all know from Sci-Fi and action movies, there are many superheroes with different skills and competencies living in different universes. Sometimes they face challenges on their own, or together as a team, depending on the task and goal. Let’s start with our embedded superheroes in the cloud.

In the SAP world, one of our embedded superheroes, who makes our daily life easier, is called Joule. Joule is a generative AI assistant and revolutionizes how customers can interact with their SAP business systems. For us there is no doubt, Joule’s impact will be huge. Not only for the business users but also for developers and how they can create clean-core compliant SAP extensions with i.e. SAP Build Code. Of course, there is not only a generative AI superhero in our embedded universe, but also classical AI use cases directly embedded into the business process. For example, there are predefined predictive use cases, like the Cash App, which can be embedded into the process in SAP S/4HANA Cloud. If the use case fits the customer’s needs, they train the predefined models with their own data and are ready to use the advanced technology in their application. So, customers can train the embedded superhero with specific skills required in their situation. An extensive summary of all the available embedded use cases for the different industries is available under the following link. 

To make these AI use cases possible we need a strong (technical) foundation for our superheroes. This is where they gain all their superhero powers. Often, customers want to embed their own AI use cases into their business processes. Hence, SAP offers the AI Foundation powered by the Business Technology Platform. The AI Foundation consists i.e. of prepared AI Business services, HANA Machine Learning & Vector Database, SAC Smart Predict, AI Core & Launchpad and a capability of AI Core called the Generative AI Hub.

Let’s start with our SAP AI Business Services consisting of the Document Information Extraction service, Data Attribute Recommendation service and Personalized Recommendation service. For example, through the Document Information Extraction service customers can extract entities from unstructured data and integrate them into the process. This service has become very flexible by adding generative AI capabilities. I encourage you to give this a try in the BTP Trial edition following these tutorials. The Data Attribute Recommendation service can be a life saver if there are missing values in the dataset. Nothing can be more frustrating! Customers can train this service on their own data following this developer tutorial.

What is a superhero without a mission? Business context is key to bring an AI model into production. Even the most popular data science process (CRISP-DM) starts with the business understanding. In HANA Cloud and SAP Datasphere, customers can keep the business context of their data. Even better through HANA Machine Learning SAP brought the algorithms to the data. Classification, regression, time series analysis, clustering, and many more statistical methods are available directly where the SAP data resides. If you are curious, give this hands-on tutorial a try. Also, the business user can use a variety of these algorithms through Smart Predict in SAP Analytics Cloud. SAP Analytics Cloud is our self-service tool for business intelligence, predictive analytics, and planning. Further, the planer can even combine the predictive and planning capabilities. In addition, Just Ask enables the user to query their data with natural language powered by AI. Stay tuned because there is more to come, link.

AI Core and AI Launchpad enable the developer and ML engineers to deploy their own models inside of the Business Technology Platform and integrate them with other SAP solutions. If you want to create your first AI project using AI Core, have a look at the following developer tutorial. Sometimes our local superheroes need help from other universes, therefore you can access different Large Language Models (LLMs) through the Generative AI Hub. Since the general knowledge of these models is limited to a certain point in time, these superhero’s might not understand the language of your business universe. To help here, SAP has announced a Vector Engine as part of HANA Cloud. With the Vector Engine, customers can embed company-specific information into the knowledge of LLMs and can use the power of generative AI in the specific corporate context. If you want to dig deeper, have a look at the following blog post.

In addition, there are many more partnerships which complement the SAP portfolio. Other vendors offer very powerful data science solutions, and the integration can support data scientists to bring more AI models into production. For example, have a look at the following reference architecture.

Do you face any challenges that require an AI superhero to solve? Let us know!

We introduced our superheroes to you and how different user groups are enabled to combine AI with their business. This is our vision of saving the business world.

I want to thank Holger Seubert and Prof. Dr. Sarah Detzler for their support in writing this blog post!

Best wishes

Yannick

entry.title: User Experience Advances with SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS01 (Private Cloud and On-Premise)
entry.content:
The new proactive “My Home” page is now available via Feature Pack Stack 1 (FPS01) for SAP S/4HANA 2023. This release also allows customers to merge spaces and pages for a better user experience, and provides further cool ways to collaborate with Microsoft Teams. Beyond that, we’ve included further improvements in the user experience, including updates to the Horizon visual theme. Last but not least, SAP Start can now be integrated to the private cloud, and you can get an outlook on our plans for integrating Joule.

This post gives you an overview of user experience innovations relevant for SAP S/4HANA since the initial release last October: innovations which came with FPS01 as well as relevant design and technology updates.

If you haven’t already seen it, have a look at this post for the overview of what came with the initial 2023 release:

  • SAP User Experience Update: What’s New for SAP S/4HANA 2023 (Private Cloud and On-Premise).

To see how good SAP S/4HANA Cloud, private edition, now looks across all the main product areas I thoroughly recommend that you have a look at the online Product Tour.

New My Home Page now available

We have delivered as we had promised in October: the intuitive and powerful new My Home product home page with SAP S/4HANA 2023 Cloud, private edition and SAP S/4HANA 2023 (on-premise) is available now. This builds on the spaces and pages functionality of the SAP Fiori launchpad, so if you have not yet moved from the launchpad’s classic home page, which uses groups, now is the time to get started!

Figure 1 shows the main features of this new start page, and the subsequent four-minute video lets you watch it in action: how to use it, as well as how to personalize it. My Home benefits from extensive feedback from users of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, where it was first introduced over a year ago, and which has had three updates since then.

Figure 1: The new My Home product home page.

The new My Home is an easy-to-use product home page that supports business users in their everyday tasks and optimizes their work environment significantly. It orchestrates information, tasks, and applications so that you can access exactly what you need to get your job done with speed and agility.

Combining items from the ‘My Inbox’ and ‘My Situations’ SAP Fiori apps, it enables you to access and manage your to-dos and favorite pages and apps. Insights offer a particularly powerful capability: users can personalize which insight cards they want to have on the My Home page, by choosing from cards in their overview page apps, or by creating cards directly from SAP Fiori elements list report applications. In addition, the news section allows administrators to provide users with important news, and users can personalize the selection of topics.

The details matter if you want to provide a great user experience: Figure 2 shows how user feedback has led us to provide an option to refresh all the Insight Tiles with one click. We provide the same option for refreshing all the Insight Cards with one click too.

Figure 2: My Home offers a menu for managing sections, pages, and insight cards directly next to the respective title, with a one-click refresh option for everything within an insight section.

Another feature which we added based on user feedback, which is shown right at the end of the video below, is the option for users to share their personalized My Home definition with colleagues, by exporting to a file, which each colleague can then import. This means that a key user could define an optimum My Home for their work and share with all the other colleagues in the team who have the same roles.

Video 1: Look at the new My Home product home page, and how users can personalize it to their needs.

Note that the above video is taken from an SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2402 system, which includes one feature which is not yet available for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, nor for SAP S/4HANA: support for direct collaboration with creators of tasks via Microsoft Teams chats or calls, described further below in the Collaboration section. We do however plan to provide this in Q4/2024. [Please note that, as with all such forward-looking statements, our plans are subject to change, but we hope to deliver as planned].

You can read more about My Home in the documentation:

  • Documentation: My Home in SAP S/4HANA.

 

Merging Spaces and Pages in the SAP Fiori Launchpad

Beyond the new My Home page, we also give customers the means to improve the user experience of the other SAP Fiori launchpad spaces and pages: customers can choose to merge spaces, pages and even sections – those from SAP as well as custom ones.

Why would you want to do that? Basically, if a user has a few spaces which don’t contain many pages, or pages which don’t have many sections, you can optimize their navigation by merging them. Users then have fewer navigation options and see more applications at a glance on one page. Also, in some cases users can end up with pages or sections with the same name, so it would make sense for users to have these merged into just one page or section.

The layout is automatically adjusted, so this can be done with low effort. Figure 3 shows an example, where two spaces (“Billing” and “Customer Returns”), each with only one page, have been merged into one space (“Billing”), and where the two pages have been merged into one page. The two sections “Customer Returns” and “Flexible Analysis” from the “Customer Returns” page are automatically added behind the section “Billing Documents and Billing Document Requests” from the “Billing” page.

Figure 3: Merging spaces and pages.

More information is available here:

  • Merging Spaces and Pages.
  • Documentation: Merging Spaces.

Composite Roles supported by Apps for Managing Spaces and Pages

Now, the Manage Launchpad Pages and the Manage Launchpad Spaces apps support composite roles. These are roles defined in SAP S/4HANA (private cloud and on-premise) which are comprised of multiple single roles.

This means that administrators who are using Manage Launchpad Pages to define pages for users with a composite role now see all the catalogs and tiles assigned to all of the single roles contained in the composite role. As a result, maintainenance is far less error-prone than before, where they had to select from the list of all tiles in all catalogs, rather than being shown the relevant subset.

 

Collaboration: New Capabilities with Microsoft Teams

Share as Card with Microsoft Teams Collaborative Stage View

You can already share a link to an application in a Microsoft Teams chat, but now you can go a step further and share the application as a card in the chat. When the recipient opens the card by clicking on the “open” button, the shared application is opened within Microsoft Teams as a new chat window, which Microsoft calls the collaborative stage view. Users can work with the application and chat with colleagues in the same chat window. Figures 4, 5 and 6 show some of these steps, but to get the best impression, watch this recording from an SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2402 system, which provides the same functionality:

  • Video: Collaborative ERP: Share as Card based on Collaborative Stage View (1:49).

Figure 4: Share as Card – showing how the user Jim selects this for his sales order.

Figure 5: Share as Card – this is what it looks like for the recipient, Alice, after Jim shared the card and later added in the chat “Hey Alice, can you please remove the delivery block? It’s urgent! Thanks.”

Figure 6: Share as Card – after Alice clicked on “Open” in the card, she sees the application in the Microsoft Teams collaborative stage view, next to the chat. In this example, she has already interacted with the app in-place and removed the delivery block, and is about to send off her reply in the chat to Jim.

Planned: Initiate a Chat Directly from a Contact Card on the UI

Wherever a person’s name or e-mail address is shown with an assigned contact card, we plan to allow users to directly start a Microsoft Teams chat or call with that person. Figure 7 shows an example from the new My Home page, showing how you will be able to immediately start a chat, video call or voice call with the person, in this case the creator of the task; you can also see an example in the above My Home video. As mentioned above, we plan to provide this feature in Q4/2024. [Please note that, as with all such forward-looking statements, our plans are subject to change].

Figure 7 (preview): Chat, start a video call or a voice-only call with the creator of a task, via Microsoft Teams (planned for Q4/2024).

 

Improvements in Details

Below I’ve chosen some of the highlights which come with FPS01. To see all the new features, look at the documentation:

  • What’s New Viewer ABAP Platform (User Experience)

Copy and Paste in SAPUI5 Tables

Users can easily select areas in SAPUI5-based tables now and copy the contents from the clipboard and then paste into other applications, such as an e-mail or a spreadsheet. The copy action can be triggered by a new button added to the table, as demonstrated in Figure 8, or via the keyboard.

Figure 8: Copy and Paste from SAPUI5 tables.

Sorting in Tables by Name, Not Just by ID

In tables, we often show the ID and the name of an entity in one column, as “ID (Name)”, rather than in two separate columns. With this release, the table sorting feature offers you the option to sort by name and not just by ID. Technically, this is a feature of the Smart Table in SAPUI5.

Figure 9: Table columns can now be sorted by name in addition to ID. In this example, the Company Code column contains values with ID “0001” and name “SAP DEMO Company”; you can now sort by either of these.

Enhanced Type Ahead for (Multi) Combo Boxes

When entering data in the system, users want to do this as quickly as possible. Being able to very quickly select an entry from a drop-down combo box or multi-combo box helps a lot, and we have now made this easier by selecting all entries which have a match anywhere with what you have typed – and not just matching the first letters. Figure 10 shows two examples for the field Credit Check Rule: the entry is selected which matches the input somewhere – and the part which matches is highlighted in bold text.

Figure 10: Enhanced type ahead functionality for combo boxes and multi-combo boxes.

Save as Tile Supports Dynamic Date Ranges

You can now create dynamic tiles for your selected SAP Fiori launchpad pages which include dynamic date ranges in the selection. Figure 11 shows an example where the date range “This Year (01.01.2023 – 31.12.2023)” has been used to filter the list. From there, the user has called up the “Save as Tile” feature, resulting in a tile which always shows the values for “This Year”.

Figure 11: Save as Tile supports dynamic date ranges.

Calendar Week Handling in User Settings

Users can now choose how they want dates to be displayed in calendars, for example when choosing a date via value-help. In addition to using the default format from your system, you can now select between ISO_8601 format, Middle Eastern and Western Traditional, as you can see in Figure 12.

Figure 12: Calendar week handling via the SAP Fiori launchpad user settings.

User ID Displayed in User Settings

I’m glad to say that we now support one of our main customer requests: users can see their technical user ID in the SAP Fiori launchpad settings section, on the tab User Account. This helps in cases where support is needed, since the technical ID is logged rather than the user’s name.

Select All in Tables

Users can now select all the visible entries in a list report, using the select all checkbox in the table header. Since users might think that all entries in the database have been selected, when in fact only the entries which have been loaded to the UI are selected, a warning pop-up is shown – as you can see in Figure 13.

Figure 13: Select all enabled by default for list reports.

Further Improvements

I would also like to mention these improvements to tables:

  • Creation rows in tables indicate to users immediately if mandatory values are missing.
  • Creation rows in tables allow developers to disable fields which should not be open for entering data due to missing entries in other fields.

 

UI Design News

Design Improvements to the Horizon Visual Theme for SAP Fiori

Another area where we have been listening to user feedback is our current Horizon visual theme, which we first introduced two years ago to offer you a fresh and modern SAP Fiori experience and to help you get your job done faster, more intuitively, and with better outcomes. Taking your feedback to heart, we’ve improved a number of details such as:

  • Improved contrast;
  • White input fields instead of gray;
  • Clearly identifiable buttons;
  • Reduced header size;
  • And more.

Figure 14 summarizes most of these updates in one example UI. You can read more about this and look at more detailed examples in my blog post from September, where I gave an outlook on these planned improvements, which have now become available:

  • Improvements Coming to the Horizon Visual Theme for SAP Fiori

Figure 14: A summary of the updates to the Horizon visual theme, using the Flexible Column Layout pattern as an example to show what it used to look like before the update and what it now looks like after the update.

Web Design Guidelines for SAP Fiori Updated: GenAI and SAP Web Components

We have revamped the design guidelines for SAP Fiori for web, with a first set of guidelines in the new subsection for designing for Generative AI in the Designing Intelligent Systems section. It covers:

  • Design Principles for Generative AI.
  • Building Trust in Generative AI.

We are releasing this early version of our generative AI design guidelines to share our discoveries early on with our broader SAP and UX community. The design guidelines in this section will evolve iteratively based on research, input from the community, and feedback from stakeholders.

Coming soon:

  • Designing Safety into Generative AI
  • Designing Sustainable Generative AI Experiences
  • Designing Effective AI Prompts

In 2H2023 we also provided a beta release of a new section UI Elements – SAP Web Components, where you can interact directly with each web component, since they have been embedded into the respective guideline page.

Mobile News

We have enhanced the design of SAP Mobile Start, which can now also run on Apple Vision Pro, as well as enhancing the mobile design system. Find out more in these blog posts:

  • Exciting new features with SAP Mobile Start 1.9, including new iOS 17 features.
  • Apple Vision Pro is available – SAP Mobile Start V1.10 is ready.
  • SAP Fiori for Android 7.1 and iOS 10.0 – Introducing New Components and Mobile Card System.

 

SAP Start with SAP S/4HANA 2023 Cloud Private Edition

The purpose of SAP Start is to bring together all truly relevant business information for each user in one central place in their web browser, and also to provide the one place to go to start your SAP applications in the cloud, no matter which product you want to use. SAP Start:

  • Gives you quick access to all your applications via the simple yet powerful search field;
  • Proactively informs you about your to-dos from all products which are connected to the SAP Task Center;
  • Gives you key insights via insight cards from products connected to SAP Start,
  • Assists you with Joule, your copilot for working with SAP.

As a result: SAP Start will be the best place to start, to simplify your access to multiple SAP cloud products.

Now, with FPS01, you can integrate SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2023 with SAP Start. To find out more, have a look at the documentation:

  • What is SAP Start?
  • Integrate SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition.

 

What about Joule?

Using natural language to ask for something is… natural! In other words: a very powerful way to interact with systems. With Joule, we are using the power of GenAI to give you a copilot for your interactions with SAP. It is generally available already today with SAP Start as well as with SAP SuccessFactors, providing an initial set of capabilities, and is available for selected customers of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2402 via an Early Adopter Care program. You can find out more about how it is being used in these products here:

  • SAP UX Innovations in Public Cloud Products, Joule, AI, Entry Points, Apple Vision Pro and more.

What about Joule for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition? Although the next release is planned for 2025, we do plan to incrementally start integrating Joule with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2023 in the course of this year – so keep your eyes peeled for updates about this! [Please note that, as with all such forward-looking statements, our plans are subject to change, but we hope to deliver as planned]. 

If you’d like to get an idea of what this might look like, have a look at what we are working on for the Early Adopter Care program for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. This presentation explains the three types of patterns (Navigational, Transactional and Informational) as well as providing an overview of our AI approach in general and a high-level description of the Joule technology stack:

  • PDF download link: Joule, the AI copilot in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

This video shows examples of using Joule with all three interaction patterns:

  • Video: Joule in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition - Early Adopter Care in 2402.

 

Final Words

Do keep posting your experiences and recommendations yourself in the SAP Community, with the SAP Fiori and/or the User Experience tag! In case you are wondering how to get a list of the most recent blog posts on SAP Fiori and User Experience in our new SAP Community, use these links:

  • Latest blog posts on SAP Fiori in the SAP Community.
  • Latest blog posts on User Experience in the SAP Community.

For general information on design at SAP and SAP Fiori, check out:

  • www.sap.com/design.
  • SAP Community: SAP Fiori topic page.

entry.title: SAPinsider 2024: Are You Ready for a Challenge?
entry.content:
With SAPinsider 2024 Las Vegas just one week away, it’s now time to start planning your agenda for this annual extravaganza of topics related to SAP. As usual, you can expect to hear from many speakers from SAP, SAP Business Partners as well as SAP customers, on a broad range of topics related to SAP technologies and solutions, intended to keep you right up to date on the latest developments, tech trends and best practices related to the software that you use to run your business.

But this year there’s a difference. There’s a challenge ahead for those brave enough to take it. And if you’re a tech enthusiast, programmer, or software developer with an interest in AI then this challenge could be right up your street. But more about that soon…first let's talk about sessions!

As you probably know from my previous blogs, my interest relates primarily to SAP BTP as well as AI, and so before I treat you to details about the challenge, I’ll first share my thoughts on some excellent sessions that I think will appeal to anyone with an interest in BTP and AI topics too. Let’s start with my 6 top picks for Vegas ’24:

  1. Hands-on Lab: Harness SAP Datasphere as an Enterprise-Wide Data Fabric,19 March, 10:30am to 12:30pm
  2. Case Study: Exploring Johnson & Johnson's SAP BTP Implementation Path to Industrialization from Groundwork, 19 March, 2:10pm to 2:50pm
  3. Case Study: Automating the Concur Receipt Download Using SAP BTP-Integration Suite and SAP Build Process Automation, 20 March, 1:10pm to 1:50pm
  4. Transform planning and analytics with Joule, SAP’s generative AI copilot, 20 March, 2:10pm to 2:50pm
  5. Case Study: Vision to Reality: Hanesbrands's Journey to SAP Business Technology Platform and SAP Datasphere, 20 March, 3:30pm to 4:10pm
  6. SAP Business Technology Platform and Analytics Use Cases and Success Strategies, 20 March, 4:30pm to 5:10pm

Of course, this is just to whet your appetite as there’s plenty more to see. Hop on to the SAPinsider 2024 website and look at the event sessions list, or even better download the SAPinsider2024 event app to your smart device (available on the App Store and Google Play) to see a full list of available sessions, and to create your own personal agenda.

Deep Dive into SAP BTP for a Day

The first of two new exciting activities planned for SAPinsider 2024 this year, both of which take place on the event pre-conference day, 18 March, is the SAP BTP Leadership Exchange, which offers a day-long informative experience followed by a networking reception, where you’ll enjoy hearing SAP BTP customer stories and topical sessions designed to explore the potential for BTP and AI to help organizations innovate faster and maximize business value from their IT Investments. This is a special invitation-only event, but if you’re interested in this event and want to network with your peers then there’s still time to apply for an invitation by completing the form on the event landing page. I hope to see you there!

 

A Challenge Awaits

Our second new activity this year is the Developer’s Challenge Using Artificial Intelligence, in which up to 10 teams will compete, each collaborating on a solution for a development project which incorporates AI. The top 3 solutions will be presented the following day at the SAPinsider conference, and the winner announced at the conference closing keynote session on that same day, Tuesday, March 19, 2024. Designed for the technical audience and developers attending the event, we’re hoping that this will offer you an opportunity to flex your problem-solving skills, creativity and AI knowledge, in a competitive, but fun activity. If this appeals to you then there’s still time to sign up for what’s sure to be a stimulating day. Good luck. I’m excited to see the winning entries!

 

Share Your Own Experience of SAP BTP

My final call-out, and possibly one of our most popular show floor activities, SAP shall again be offering our customers the opportunity to leave reviews about their experiences with SAP solutions. If you’d like to leave a review, then please visit the team at exhibit booth #615 and complete a quick and simple 10-minute survey with our friends from TrustRadius.

And, even if you’re not attending SAPinsider 2024 in Las Vegas then that’s not a problem. You can still participate and submit a product review for SAP BTP or another SAP solution by visiting our SAP Product Review page.

Thank you for taking the time to read this blog. I hope that you’re as excited as I am about SAPinsider 2024. My best wishes for safe travels to Las Vegas next week, and I hope that you enjoy the conference!

Chris Grundy

Product Marketing, SAP BTP

entry.title: 10+ ways to reshape your SAP landscape with SAP Business Technology Platform – Blog Series
entry.content:
Blog 2: The role of Business Technology Platform as an extension and innovation platform

This blog is part of the “10+ ways to reshape your SAP landscape with SAP Business Technology Platform Blog Series”

Summary: In this blog series we will look at the role of the SAP Business Technology Platform to reshape your SAP landscape including SAP and Non-SAP systems. In this second blog we discuss SAP Business Technology Platform capabilities around process automation, application development, enterprise integration, and AI. And how these capabilities support the most demanding businesses. 

 

What does Mercedes AMG Petronas Formula One have in common with SAP Business Technology Platform? -- Tons of it. 

Just like any other sophisticated business, Mercedes-AMG PETRONAS Formula One is in the cutthroat business of racing. And it competes in one of the most challenging environments in global sports. Combining elite teamwork, modern technologies, high-performance management, and exceptional driving skills, Formula One is a technologically innovative form of motorsport, where incremental gains yield significant results and can be the difference between winning and losing. 

So what is common with Business Technology Platform? 

The Formula One team has to compete in 22-24 different race tracks during a season. Each race track presents a different set of challenges, which can only be mastered when all the data is well understood. Data is what drives strategy in Formula One. Strategy is based on a set of decisions made by track engineers, drivers, and strategists to try to maximize the result in the race and qualifying by optimizing the tools at their disposal to make important calls such as tire decisions, the timing and amount of pit stops, and the management of the cars in the race. And all the decisions for each of the scenarios require intensive data analysis. We will cover data management and analytics in Blog 11: The role of Data & Analytics in the transformation by Wolfgang Epting.

The challenges above are similar to those experienced by our customers where they have to navigate changing market situations and customer demands, and analyse and respond to dynamic requirements, all while managing quality, cost, navigating through processes, and time to market. 

In this blog, I want to emphasize the Business Technology Platform, which brings cutting-edge cloud services around application development, process automation, enterprise integration, analytics, and AI, all of which should help IT deliver fully integrated rapid enhancements. The value of AI and Gen AI for business increases exponentially when combined with industry-specific data and deep process knowledge, and this is exactly what SAP does. Joule, the generative AI assistant, revolutionizes how you interact with SAP business systems, making every touchpoint count and every task simpler. More on AI later from Mark on Blog 4: The Role of AI in the transformation. Stay tuned for that.

With the switch to S/4HANA, business can build their competitive car on a new chassis.  The focus is on cloud first strategy and clean core, i.e. a largely untouched SAP core. BTP is used to develop, provide, and scale new applications in the cloud. Add-on solutions on the BTP can therefore be easily connected to SAP S/4HANA, while the SAP system remains 100% clean.

Let us look at some specific areas.

Formula One has long had active aero on cars – the rear-wing-opening overtaking aid, DRS, for instance. This is part of the aero package. Obviously, the extremes would be Monza/Spa for low downforce, and Monaco/Hungary for highest. With Silverstone as maybe the ultimate “compromise” setup on the less draggy side but also needs good high-speed downforce, especially for Copse/Maggotts/Becketts where the driver experiences lateral forces upto 6Gs during acceleration, braking and cornering.  

Quite the same way, SAP Business Technology Platform, is the best path to rapidly build and enhance enterprise applications, processes, and experiences on top of the S/HANA chassis for various industry and internal business requirements. The prime idea is to build competition-beating processes and application enhancements quickly and deliver them to business. An example is building intuitive applications for field staff that provide necessary data points and actions intending to reduce paperwork. Or using AI to generate work orders with necessary steps which allows planners to execute orders faster. A clean core enables you to become future-proof by ensuring faster deployment with smooth upgrades – all while using BTP allowing you to innovate at market speed.

Think about this as developing the rear wing of the car and delivering upgrades during the season. Teams have to find the sweet spot between elements such as endplates, flaps, gurney flaps, and DRS. Teams can make changes to optimize the car's performance on different tracks. 

From a business perspective, this is where we employ SAP Business Technology Platform BUILD capabilities. This allows you to:

  • Simplify your cloud application development to get more value and ignite innovation
  • Deliver agile process automation to automate and optimize processes and tasks
  • Connect people, data, and processes with engaging experiences

Approach it from a business angle. SAP Build is SAP’s low-code offering, designed to empower users of all skills to easily create and augment their SAP and non-SAP business applications with visual drag-and-drop, without requiring programming skills.

An example here is a shipping and logistics company that has built a Mobile App within 3 weeks, to allow drivers to capture damages to their vehicles. This app has greatly reduced tons of paperwork. This has been coupled with automation to help fleet managers plan required repairs and/or schedule them for a later date based on spare parts availability.

SAP Build brings together SAP Build Apps, SAP Build Process Automation, SAP Build Work Zone and SAP Build Code with generative AI-based code development into a unified development experience with new innovations to rapidly build apps, automate processes, and create business websites. These "four carriages" work together to help users accelerate application development and process automation, empowering every employee in the organization: 

  • Build visually: Rapidly create enterprise apps, automate processes, and design business sites. 
  • Integrate seamlessly: Accelerate development with prebuilt connectors and lines of business-specific content for SAP and non-SAP systems. 
  • Collaborate securely: Foster collaboration between business and development teams with built-in governance and lifecycle management. 

 

In addition, SAP Build Code, generative AI-based code development with Joule copilot, will provide optimized Java and JavaScript application development abilities, providing a turn-key environment for coding, testing, integrations, and application lifecycle management.

 

SAP Build Code brings:

  • Generative AI-based code development with Joule copilot - uses AI to generate app logic, data models, and test scripts.
  • Tailored for SAP development – helps you connect seamlessly with prebuilt integrations and APIs.
  • Enhanced fusion development – helps you drive collaboration between professional developers and business experts.

 

Each Formula One car is made up of around 14,500 individual components. Each element such as chassis, power unit, gearbox, aero package, braking systems, battery, cooling systems, telemetry, radio communications, tires, driver, etc. have to be truly well integrated to ensure the car is ready to race, is reliable and will win.

Similarly, in business, systems in isolation will never work and provide a full picture of business. They have to be integrated with core systems, and external applications, leverage 3rd party data and provide all of these integrated insights to business race engineers, and enable them to make race-winning decisions.

Hence SAP Integration Suite is essential to integrate on-premises and cloud-based processes, services, applications, events, and data and provide them securely to all stakeholders to consume and process information. This is where we offer SAP BTP Integration Suite which addresses hybrid integration strategies. 

The strategy to support innovation is clear.

  • Accelerate innovation and integrate without obstacles
  • Speed connectivity and realize value faster
  • Connect customers and workers to automated processes
  • Conquer process fragmentation and drive productivity
  • Streamline workflows and improve the employee experience 
  • Connect with partner systems and improve collaboration 

Top nine ways SAP Integration Suite drives innovation

BTP Integration Suite therefore offers: 

  • Prebuilt integrations managed and updated by SAP
  • Harmonized access to popular third-party cloud applications
  • Tools for designing, publishing, and managing APIs
  • AI-assisted development and integration optimization
  • Tools-based, guided approach to define, document, and govern your integration strategy 
  • Edge Integration Cell to run integration scenarios within customer-managed private landscapes for on-prem to on-prem workloads.

 

The new world of innovation through Generative AI via the SAP perspective.

I would equate the race engineer to Gen AI today. Simply because today, race engineers are crucial assets in every Formula One team, as they are the intelligence who ensure drivers and their vehicles are attuned to put on a show on the track. They analyze and call on all data points. I would use the parallels where the car is business applications, drivers as users, track and weather data is external data, car data is business data, custom setups during races as Gen AI content and radio communications as prompts for business users.  

SAP Business AI is aimed at making our business applications more intelligent, infusing AI technologies into our applications. Furthermore, we provide business process-specific AI services that our customers can adapt to their workflow, e.g., business document processing, data attribute recommendation, RPA, etc.

Building data understanding is essential for the success of business AI. SAP has access to business data covering finance, supply chain, human resources, travel, and tons of external data relevant for business.

To make business AI successful, we continue to build the best technology and leverage the best tools on the market.

  • This includes strategic partnerships with others in the industry, such as Open AI, AWS Bedrock, Aleph Alpha Luminous, Meta Llama 2, Falcon, etc.
  • Embed their tech into our products such as SuccessFactors, S4, CX and Spend Manegement.
  • Enable customers to build custom ML models on SAP data easily and securely.

To conclude SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) is the platform of choice to modernize, upgrade, and drive innovations in your applications. It allows you to extend and personalize SAP applications, integrate and connect landscapes, and unleash business users to connect processes and experiences, make decisions with confidence, and drive business innovation.

Let’s get out there and win every race.

 

The Blog Series 10+ ways to reshape your SAP landscape with SAP Business Technology Platform will be published regularly and the newest blogs can be found here: Video & Blog Series - SAP BTP MEE Overview (brandcast.io)

To get more insights, please also visit one of our BTP Innovation Days:

  • Innovation Day Vienna:                 Vienna 04-Apr-24             SAP BTP Innovation Day Vienna
  • Innovation Day Switzerland:         Bern 14-May-24                SAP | SAP BTP Innovation Day Switzerland
  • Innovation Day Germany              Essen 16-May-24              SAP | SAP BTP Innovation Day

entry.title: Announcing General Availability of SAP Build Code: Speed up development with generative AI
entry.content:
SAP is dedicated to increasing developer productivity enabling rapid extension of SAP S/4HANA and other enterprise applications. To support this mission, we announced SAP Build Code at TechEd 2023 and today, I’m excited to announce that it is generally available to all developers. Get started with the Test Drive tutorial!

What is SAP Build Code?

SAP Build Code provides AI-based code generation with Joule copilot, optimized for Java and JavaScript application development. It is a comprehensive, turn-key solution for coding, testing, integrations, and application lifecycle management. You can leverage Joule to efficiently generate application logic, data models, and test scripts. As an end-to-end integrated solution, SAP Build Code enhances the entire development process, from design time to runtime, significantly boosting your productivity.

Picture 1: Joule-powered code generation and visual representation of the code in SAP Build Code 

SAP Build Code is tailored for SAP development, allowing you to utilize your favorite SAP programming models and frameworks and enabling you to connect seamlessly to S/4HANA and other applications with prebuilt integrations. It provides you with a guided development experience following best practices from SAP. The Service Center in SAP Build Code provides direct access to discovery and consumption of SAP APIs Including OData and Business Events from SAP and third-party systems. As an integral part of SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP), SAP Build inherits all the robust security and integration capabilities of BTP. This not only enhances security but also simplifies the development of extensions and applications for SAP S/4HANA and other enterprise applications following SAPs clean core strategy.

 Picture 2: Direct access to SAP applications and data in SAP Build Code through the Service Center 

But there's more – SAP Build Code encourages fusion development, an approach that uses a mix of traditional coding and low-code development. This is thanks to its support for app composability and the reuse of project components across SAP Build low-code solutions and ABAP environments. SAP Build Code makes your development process faster, more collaborative, and more integrated with the SAP ecosystem, perfect for those looking to streamline their development projects.

What developers are saying about SAP Build Code

Since announcing the solution, we’ve had 450 developers use SAP Build Code in our preview program. Here are a couple of my favorite quotes from the developers who have used SAP Build Code to build full-stack SAP Fiori applications so far:

"SAP Build Code is yet another great offering within the SAP BTP ecosystem. I'm impressed by its maturity and developer-friendly features that quickly get you up and running. Gone are the days of wrestling with your application logic and data models. With a thoughtful prompt, Joule can get your application logic and data models created and fully functioning in just a few clicks."
– Mark Smith, Senior Full-Stack Developer, Mindset Consulting

“SAP Build Code will enable rapid development and has all the features we require to build a full stack solution powered with generative AI, including pre-configured runtime and design time capabilities, enabling shorter lead time to market.”
– Nilesh Mhatre, CIO Americas, IMEA and Global Technology Center, Henkel

Try it yourself – take SAP Build Code for a Test Drive!

You can experience SAP Build Code with the Test Drive tutorial. This unique opportunity allows you to explore the full capabilities of SAP Build Code, including Joule-powered AI-based code generation, in the SAP BTP Trial, guided by the expertise of SAP Developer Advocates and the vibrant SAP Community.

By participating, you will not only gain hands-on experience with SAP Build Code but also have the chance to earn an SAP Community badge, recognizing your efforts and learning. This is an excellent way to enhance your skills, connect with fellow developers, and get a head start with SAP Build Code.
